---
about:
  template: jolla
  id: about-block
  image: "img/my_image.jpeg"
  links:
    - icon: twitter
      text: Bluesky
      href: https://bsky.app/profile/olafborghi.com
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/olafborghi
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/olaf-borghi-864276280/
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: "mailto:olaf.borghi@rhul.ac.uk"
engine: knitr
freeze: false
---

```{=html}
<style>
#title-block-header { display: none !important; }
main.content { padding-top: 0 !important; margin-top: 0 !important; }
.quarto-about-jolla,
.about-entity,
#about-block { padding-top: 0 !important; margin-top: 0 !important; }
.quarto-about-jolla .about-image { margin-top: 0 !important; }
</style>
```

```{=html}
<div class="home-hero">
  <img src="img/new_logo.svg" class="hero-logo" alt="OB Logo">
  <div class="hero-text">
    <div class="hero-name">Olaf Borghi, MSc</div>
    <div class="hero-subtitle">Doctoral Candidate in Psychology</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

::: {#about-block}
:::

I am a doctoral candidate in the Marie-Curie network "[Interdisciplinary Perspectives of the Politics of Adolescence & Democracy](https://www.ippad.eu)" based at the Centre for the Politics of Feelings (Royal Holloway, University of London).

In my PhD, I investigate how political beliefs and attitudes develop, and the cognitive and emotional factors underlying this development. The developmental focus of my research works in two ways. First, I focus on adolescence and young adulthood – times of cognitive, emotional, and political change. Second, I study processes of belief development, examining how people change their minds based on new information, and different biases that affect political belief formation and updating. I also have a research focus on how young people feel about and imagine their future in times of multiple crises, and how this relates to their support for democracy and ideological views, including gender divides on political issues.

If you are interested about my work, would like to chat or collaborate, [contact me](mailto:olaf.borghi@rhul.ac.uk)!

#### Recent News

```{=html}
<div class="news-section">
  <div class="news-item">
    <span class="news-date">Apr 2026</span>
    <p class="news-text">My research was featured in Bridging Divides, a new initiative and podcast series from the British Red Cross. As guest in Episode 2, I had the chance to talk about belief updating in politics and beyond. <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bRmexgvjphU"><strong>Bridging Divides: Can we really change people's minds?</strong></a></p>
  </div>
  <div class="news-item">
    <span class="news-date">Nov 2025</span>
    <p class="news-text">Our paper "Mind over bias: How is cognitive control related to politically motivated reasoning?" has been published in <em>Cognition</em>. <a href="research/articles/borghi-etal-2026/"><strong>Borghi et al. (2026)</strong></a></p>
  </div>
  <div class="news-item">
    <span class="news-date">Oct 2025</span>
    <p class="news-text">The first paper of my PhD, "Facing a dark future: Young people's future anxiety and political attitudes in the UK and Greece", has been published in a special issue on the Psychology of Pushback in <em>Advances.in/Psychology</em>. <a href="research/articles/borghi-etal-2025/"><strong>Borghi et al. (2025)</strong></a></p>
  </div>
</div>
```

#### Methodological interests and open science

My work has a strong methodological and interdisciplinary focus. I combine quantitative methods and tools from across the social, developmental, and cognitive sciences, including cognitive tasks, survey experiments, text analysis, and cognitive modelling. I also place a strong emphasis on open science, and commit to openly sharing data and materials for all my papers.

```{=html}
<div  style="margin: 30px; text-align: center;">
<a class="btn btn-primary" href="https://www.ippad.eu/" role="button" target="_blank" style="padding: 15px 30px;">IP-PAD MSCA Doctoral Network</a>
</div>
```
